edible_parts: Bulb (edible only after long roasting); soap/fiber raw.
other_uses: California 'amole' with a wavy-edged leaf rosette and airy evening-opening white flowers.
prep: The bulb is edible only after slow pit-roasting (raw it is a soap and a fish poison); the fibrous bulb coat makes brushes, the crushed raw bulb makes lather, and the sap a glue. A true multi-use plant.
risks: Raw bulb is a soap/fish-poison (saponins) — edible only well-roasted.
buy: Native-plant nurseries and native seed suppliers.
